channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Next Iran-US nuclear talks will be held in Rome, AP source says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Talks between Iran and the United States over Tehran's rapidly advancing nuclear program appeared ready Monday to leave the Middle East, as an Italian source said the next round of negotiations would take place in Rome.
item.dc:creator: MARIA GRAZIA MURRU and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Suspect in arson at Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro's residence planned to beat him, documents say
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A man who authorities said scaled an iron security fence in the middle of the night, eluded police and broke into the Pennsylvania governor’s mansion where he set a fire had planned to beat Gov.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pennsylvania governor's mansion attack suspect Cody Balmer admitted 'harboring hatred' toward Shapiro: police
item.description: Cody Balmber, 38, allegedly made homemade "Molotov cocktails" that he threw into the mansion Gov. Josh Shapiro and family live in early Sunday morning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspected US airstrikes kill at least 7 people in Yemen, Houthi rebels say
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes around Yemen's rebel-held capital killed at least seven people and wounded 29 overnight, the Houthis said Monday as they also claimed shooting down another American MQ-9 Reaper drone.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Plans to celebrate America's 250th anniversary were underway. Then came the federal funding cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Community celebrations being planned to commemorate the nation's 250th anniversary next year are at risk of being significantly scaled back or canceled because of federal funding cuts under President Donald Trump's administration, according to multiple state humanities councils acr
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH and GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Meta faces historic antitrust trial that could force it to break off Instagram, WhatsApp
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. faces a historic antitrust trial beginning Monday that could force the tech giant to break off Instagram and WhatsApp, startups it bought more than a decade ago that have since grown into social media powerhouses.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY AP Technology Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Majority Leader John Thune's 'old-fashioned' approach to the Senate has kept Trump on board so far
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate, once again, was working into the early morning hours Friday with its new majority leader, Republican John Thune, setting the pace. It wasn’t until just after 2 a.m.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US northern border faces Great Lakes threat from ‘bad actors’ pressing waterfront weaknesses: expert
item.description: An expert predicted that amid the White House's crackdown on illegal immigration on the southern border, bad actors will naturally turn to the northern border.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A former Colorado sheriff's deputy is to be sentenced in the death of Christian Glass
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A former Colorado sheriff’s deputy convicted in the shooting death of a 22-year-old man in distress who called 911 for help is set to be sentenced Monday.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Military schools offer test case for Trump education reforms
item.description: Military academies could increasingly show what President Trump wants to see from public schools and colleges.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Missing Shreveport 12-year-old: Louisiana police ask for public's help
item.description: 12-year-old Zyra'Kem Copeland was last seen on Saturday, April 12. He was riding a red bicycle when he left.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Woods, NewsNation

item.title: 3 cases of rare brain disease reported in Oregon
item.description: Three cases of a rare brain disease have been reported by public health officials in Hood River County.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Flanigan, ABC 4

item.title: Trump 'fully fit' to serve as commander in chief, his doctor says after recent physical
item.description: In a report released Sunday, the doctor said in a summary that Trump is “fully fit to execute the duties of Commander-in-Chief and Head of State.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A beloved pet tortoise is reunited with its family weeks after disappearing in a Mississippi tornado
item.description: KOKOMO, Miss. (AP) — Myrtle, a cherished pet tortoise, has been reunited with its family in Mississippi weeks after disappearing during a deadly tornado outbreak in March. “He’s been through a lot,” said Myrtle's grateful owner, Tiffany Emanuel.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE BATES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FAA says company whose sightseeing chopper crashed, killing 6, is ceasing operations immediately
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration says the helicopter tour company whose sightseeing chopper crashed in New York, killing six people, is shutting down operations immediately.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Search continues for Las Vegas veterinarian who apologized for kicking horse
item.description: Earlier this month, Dr. Frehner apologized for kicking a horse he was hired to give shots to. Days later, he was reported missing in the Lake Mead Recreation Area.
item.dc:creator: Madina Ansary, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Understanding mutual funds: A beginner's guide to investing
item.description: Despite recent stock market slumps and fears in the investment world, it's never a bad time to learn about strategies to boost your investing experience.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US still won't say whether it will return mistakenly deported man, despite Supreme Court decision
item.description: The Trump administration is doubling down on its decision not to tell a federal court whether it has any plans to repatriate a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported last month and remains confined in a notorious prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trans 'Zizian' cult suspect dragged from court after shouting allegations of de-transition, murder: report
item.description: A member of a radical transgender vegan cult suspected in the attempted murder of a California landlord claimed in court that guards tried to de-transition her.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An emboldened anti-abortion faction wants women who have abortions to face criminal charges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As Kristan Hawkins, president of the national anti-abortion group Students for Life, tours college campuses, she has grown accustomed to counterprotests from abortion rights activists.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYC helicopter crash in Hudson River shines light on sightseeing dangers
item.description: New York City has seen a long list of helicopter accidents in recent years, with the deadly crash in the Hudson River being the latest in a series of tragedies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mikal Mahdi, South Carolina inmate convicted in two separate 2004 murders, executed by firing squad
item.description: Mikal Mahdi, 42, was executed by the state of South Carolina on April 11 for the violent killing of off-duty Orangeburg Public Safety Officer James Myers in 2004.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about activist Mahmoud Khalil and his attorneys' plan to appeal his deportation ruling
item.description: JENA, La. (AP) — An immigration judge has ruled that a Palestinian Columbia University graduate student who participated in protests against Israel can be deported. Mahmoud Khalil's attorneys said they will appeal Friday's ruling.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Luigi Mangione asks judge to block Bondi from seeking death penalty
item.description: Luigi Mangione, who is accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson last year, asked a federal judge to block Attorney General Pam Bondi from seeking the death penalty against him.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Trump admin adds migrant Social Security numbers to 'death list' in deportation push
item.description: The request from Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem to place the migrants on the “Ineligible Master File” maintained by the SSA would bar migrants from receiving benefits or being able to work in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Judge rules Menendez brothers’ bid for freedom through resentencing can continue
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Erik and Lyle Menendez’s resentencing hearings can continue despite opposition from the Los Angeles County district attorney, a judge ruled Friday.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Divers search for parts after NYC helicopter crash killed a family of five and the pilot, a Navy vet
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Divers using sonar searched Friday for key pieces of a sightseeing helicopter that broke apart in midair and plunged into the Hudson River between Manhattan and New Jersey. All six people aboard were killed — a family of five from Spain and the pilot, a 36-year-old U.S. Navy veteran.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, DAVE COLLINS and TED SHAFFREY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Augusta man attends 62 Masters in a row
item.description: For many people, they’ll wait their whole life to go to The Masters. For others, they’ve been going their whole life.
item.dc:creator: Karlton Clay, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he would respect Supreme Court decision to return wrongly deported man
item.description: ABOARD AIR FORCE ONE — President Trump said Friday he would respect a Supreme Court decision to bring back a Maryland man the administration mistakenly deported to El Salvador. “If the Supreme Court said bring somebody back I would do that.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration adds immigrant Social Security numbers to 'death list' in deportation push
item.description: The Trump administration directed the Social Security Administration to place more than 6,300 migrants on its list for tracking dead people, blocking their ability to work in a move it hopes will encourage them to “self-deport.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Gas explosion causes $3 million in damages at Spanish Fork dental office
item.description: A gas explosion caused an estimated $3 million in damages at a Spanish Fork dental office Friday morning, according to Spanish Fork Fire & EMS.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, ABC 4

item.title: Schools lined up for help getting cleaner school buses. Then came the EPA freeze
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Looking to cut pollution from its fleet of diesel-burning school buses, an Oklahoma school district last year doubled its electric vehicles.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and ALEXA ST. JOHN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Immigration judge allows Mahmoud Khalil deportation effort to proceed
item.description: A judge in former Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil's case ruled against him Friday, finding he is eligible for deportation while giving his legal team a little less than two weeks to respond.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump announces $600 million in new deals with five law firms
item.description: The deals bring to nine the number of firms that have struck agreements with Trump in the wake of numerous actions targeting major law offices.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: Here's a look at recent aircraft tragedies, mishaps and close calls
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Six people were killed Thursday in the latest string of aviation disasters after a sightseeing helicopter broke apart and crashed into the Hudson River between New York City and New Jersey. The crash comes at a time of heightened scrutiny of aircraft collisions and near-misses.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Could Trump's tariffs slow emissions? Sure, experts say, but at great cost overall
item.description: President Donald Trump’s sweeping global tariffs have stirred widespread anxiety about a severe economic downturn -- and curiosity, for some, about how it might affect the world’s warming climate.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Greenland space base commander removed after Vance criticism
item.description: The Trump administration removed the Greenland space base commander after learning she sent an email with a slight against the vice president.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5
